rulings.Case Title: GEORGE J. GROVER JR. et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO INDIVIDUALLY AND et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 8, 2007, LINE 4. DEFENDANTS TIMOTHY FELTON, PHILLIP WILLIAMSON, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND CLAUDIA DAVISON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. There are triable issues of fact, based on the competing expert declarations, on the issue of causation. The objections to the declarations of Doctors Fischman and Murphy are overruled. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. JOHN ARTHUR SEBASTINELLI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Special Motion To Strike (Doe Deft From 3rd Amended Complaint) & Request For Fees & Costs
rulings.Rulings: DEFENDANT MICHELMAN & ROBINSON, LLP'S Special Motion To Strike (Doe Defendant From Third Amended Complaint) & Request For Fees & Costs IS Denied. Assuming defendant has established its initial burden of proof, plaintiff has set forth facts establishing that plaintiff has a probability of prevailing. Causes of action 4 through 12 are viable as to this defendant absent evidence to the contrary. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. JOHN ARTHUR SEBASTINELLI et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 8, 2007, LINE 8, DEFENDANT MICHELMAN & ROBINSON, LLP'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained as to Counts 1, 2 and 3 based on Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudie Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 C.4th 503. Plaintiff has not alleged, and it does not appear, that plaintiff can allege that this defendant owed plaintiff the requisite duty or that this defendant was an employee/agent of one owing the duty and that this defendant was acting or its own financial interest. For that reason, leave to amend is not granted. The demurrer to the remaining causes of action is overruled based on Everest Investors 8 v. Whitehall Real Estate LP XI (2002) 100 CalApp,4th 1104 where "defendant" was deemed to include does absent an express limitation in the pleading; ten days to answer. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: HOLLYWOOD PARK LAND COMPANY, LLC, A DELAWARE et al VS. BILL LOCKYER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer To Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 8, 2007, LINE 14, DEFENDANT STATE TREASURER'S OFFICE'S Demurrer To Writ Of Mandate. The demurrer is sustained as to causes of action one through 5 without leave to amend. Previously, this Court has held that this is a validation action and that is the basis for this ruling as well. Government Code § 63048.8(d) states that any suit challenging "any matter authorized by" Government Code Title 6.7, Chapter 2, Article 6.5 is subject to the validating action procedures of Section 860. The complaint challenges the validity of Government Code § 63048.63, which is one of the matters authorized by Article 6.5, and is subject to the time limitations of validation action. California Commerce Casino, Inc. v. Schwarzenegger (2007) 146 Cal.App.4th 1406. The applicable 60-day (CCP § 860) has long run. The demurrer to the 6th cause of action is overruled because it is not clear from the face of the complaint that the defendant/respondent's sole reason for responding to the public records act request as it did is based solely on the challenged statute. Ten days to answer the 6th cause of action. =(302/PJM/AL)
